danger_signals.visual_form: Ball aura/glow in opponent team color
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to score; defensive pressure required
Game state deterioration; must score more points before timer expires

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen message display ('Nice steal!' opposite behavior)
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of possession or opponent advances to free-throw line

danger_signals.visual_form: Ball bounces high and far off rim; visual rebound opportunity
danger_signals.meaning: Possession contested; vulnerable state requiring defensive positioning

safety_signals.visual_form: Ball aura/glow in player team color; player-controlled character highlighted
safety_signals.meaning: Offensive control; opportunity to advance score

safety_signals.visual_form: On-screen message 'Nice steal!' with ball transfer
safety_signals.meaning: Transition to offense; momentum shift to player team

safety_signals.visual_form: Cinematic slam dunk animation sequence; scoreboard update
Screen transition showing free-throw lineup with player at line
safety_signals.meaning: Controlled scoring opportunity; no defensive interference

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opponent team displays complementary color scheme
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player team color designates possession indicator; teammate positioning shown via colored auras

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct visual outline differentiating from player team
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opponent players rendered in different color palette; movement patterns algorithmically distinct
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Opponent team color used for ball possession aura; defensive formation indicators

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Jump ball initiation; foul call system
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Court centerline; foul messaging system; free-throw line enforcement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Title screen with 'The Star-Spangled Banner' theme (NES); intro sequence with stadium, palm trees, balloons, blimp with American flag
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players in team formation; jump ball centerline positioning; teams separated on court halves

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player-controlled character highlighted with team color aura; ball visible in character's control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can dribble, pass, shoot; teammates indicated by colored auras showing pass targets

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Opponent character highlighted; ball in opponent team color aura
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player controls defensive stance; can attempt steal, switch players, jump to block

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen focus transitions to character attempting shot; if in lane moving toward basket, cinematic dunk animation sequence triggered
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must time button release at apex of jump; ring descent visual indicates timing window for free throws

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen displays foul messaging; transition to free-throw line with five characters positioned on opposing sides of lane
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fouled player moved to free-throw line; five-second timer per attempt; ring visual descends toward basket

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: On-screen timer reaches zero; scoreboard comparison displayed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: If player score exceeds opponent: game continues with additional minute; if opponent score ≥ player score: game ends
Gameplay becomes incrementally more difficult as match duration increases; opponent AI aggression/speed increases

environmental_storytelling.example: When player character in lane moving toward basket executes shot button, screen transitions to animated slam dunk sequence; communicates moment of high-stakes scoring opportunity and athletic achievement

Side-scrolling court perspective shows both teams separated at opposite baskets; jump ball at centerline establishes neutral start state; free-throw line isolation removes defensive pressure to communicate control state
Ball and teammate indicators use team-specific color palette; instantaneously communicates possession state and available pass targets without requiring text or UI elements
Each player character moves at different speeds and jumps to different heights; visual movement patterns communicate individual player stats and require learning for optimal play strategy
NES intro sequence with stadium, oversized palm trees, balloons, and Konami logo blimp carrying American flag establishes American sporting context and legitimate athletic legitimacy

'The Star-Spangled Banner' added to NES intro (not in FDS version); establishes American cultural context for Western audiences; corrupted voice samples ('Dubble Dibble', 'Too Slow') create unintentional iconic signature

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responds to joystick + dribble button input for movement; can pass to teammates indicated by colored auras; can shoot/dunk when in appropriate zone; movement speed and jump height character-specific
Can attempt steal via steal button toward ball-carrier; can switch player control toward preferred teammate via pass button; can jump to block opposing shots when positioned near shot-taker
Indicated by colored auras showing pass availability; move in support formations based on ball position; can receive passes when targeted by player via joystick direction + pass button
Move with varying speeds based on individual stats; attempt shots; execute dunks; trigger foul system if contact with player occurs during defensive play
Attempt to block player shots; reposition defensively; can foul player character during steal attempts or defensive contact

entity_behavioral_tells.visual_tell: Aura glow in team color (player or opponent); attached to character control; shadow visible on court floor
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Follows character movement when dribbled; transfers on pass execution; follows trajectory on shot attempt; bounces visually off rim on missed shot

entity_behavioral_tells.visual_tell: Shadow visible on court; elevated above rim on missed shot
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Falls with gravity; available for rebound attempt by character positioned beneath shadow pressing shot button

notes: Double Dribble communicates game state primarily through color-coded auras (team possession indicator), cinematic animation sequences (dunk moments, free-throw transitions), character-specific movement speeds (difficulty/stat communication), and on-screen messaging ('Nice steal!'). The game lacks explicit danger/safety UI indicators; instead, state is communicated through possession aura color, timer visibility, and behavioral state changes. Regional variants (NES 'Star-Spangled Banner', intro stadium redesign, Konami logo balloon American flag addition) establish cultural/national context without affecting core manifestation mechanics. Corrupted DPCM audio samples create unintentional signature audio identity independent of visual design.
